exercises.explanation: Conceding first disarms; the counter lands harder. Pure attack sounds B2.

exercises.prompt: « Admettons. ___ que trois villes l'ont déjà fait. »
exercises.explanation: Il n'en reste pas moins que = the fact remains that — concession's counterpunch.

exercises.template: Je vous {{b1}} sur le diagnostic ; en revanche, le remède me semble pire.
exercises.explanation: Je vous rejoins sur X = I'm with you on X — then pivot with en revanche.

exercises.template: Là où je ne vous {{b1}} plus, c'est sur les chiffres.
exercises.explanation: Là où je ne vous suis plus = where you lose me (suivre, not être!).

exercises.prompt: During the entretien, the examiner is still talking. You should…

options.text: let them finish, then ask « Puis-je réagir ? »
cut in immediately — it shows passion
stay silent for the rest of the exam

exercises.explanation: Ne coupez pas la parole; a polite re-entry earns composure points.

exercises.template: Il faut {{b1}} votre position par des faits précis.
exercises.explanation: Étayer = to buttress, to support with evidence.

exercises.prompt: Match each debate move to its meaning.
Put the words in order: "I rely on facts, not on wishes."
Listen to the candidate's reply. Which moves do you hear?
exercises.transcript: Certes, le projet est ambitieux, j'en conviens ; il n'en reste pas moins que trois villes l'ont déjà mis en œuvre.

options.text: Double concession (certes, j'en conviens) then the factual counter

exercises.explanation: Concede twice, counter once with facts — textbook C1 composure.

exercises.prompt: Read this entretien transcript extract and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr:
EXAMINATEUR : Votre solution coûterait une fortune.
CANDIDATE : J'en conviens, le budget est important. Il n'en reste pas moins que l'étude que je citais l'évalue à moins d'un euro par habitant et par mois. Là où je ne vous suis plus, c'est sur le mot « fortune » : c'est le prix d'un café par trimestre.
EXAMINATEUR : Joli. C'est de bonne guerre.
exercises.passageEn:
EXAMINER: Your solution would cost a fortune.
CANDIDATE: I grant you, the budget is significant. The fact remains that the study I cited puts it at under one euro per resident per month. Where you lose me is the word "fortune": it's the price of one coffee per quarter.
EXAMINER: Nice. Fair play.

questions.prompt: How does the candidate handle the attack?

options.text: Concedes the budget, counters with cited figures, contests one word precisely

questions.prompt: « C'est de bonne guerre » signals that the examiner…

options.text: acknowledges the riposte as fair play

exercises.prompt: Why does pure attack sound B2 while concede-then-counter sounds C1?

options.text: Concession shows you weighed the other view before dismantling it

exercises.explanation: C1 argument is dialogic: integrate the objection, then go beyond it.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Certes, le coût est élevé, mais l'inaction coûterait davantage.

options.text: Admittedly the cost is high, but inaction would cost more.
I agree with your diagnosis; the remedy, however, seems worse to me.
Granted. The fact remains that three cities have already done it.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « Permettez-moi de nuancer ce constat. » — to qualify / add nuance.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.

pairs.left: là où je ne vous suis plus

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Certes, le coût est élevé, mais l'inaction coûterait davantage.
exercises.targetEn: Admittedly the cost is high, but inaction would cost more.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Admettons. Il n'en reste pas moins que trois villes l'ont déjà fait.
exercises.targetEn: Granted. The fact remains that three cities have already done it.
